questions: You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. Your manager asks you to co-lead a project with a colleague whose working style is very different from yours. How do you approach it?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. During a group brainstorm, someone suggests combining your idea with theirs into a hybrid approach. How do you respond?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. Your neighbor proposes that several households on the block coordinate to set up a shared garden. What do you say?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. A classmate in your graduate program suggests splitting up a research task so you each contribute different sections. How do you handle it?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. Your family is planning a reunion and your sibling asks everyone to each take responsibility for a different part. What do you do?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. A colleague proposes pair-programming on a tricky technical problem neither of you has solved alone. How do you react?
You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. Your community association asks for volunteers to jointly organize a neighborhood cleanup. How do you respond?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. A friend suggests you both train together for a half-marathon, adjusting your schedules to match. What do you say?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. Your team is stuck on a problem and someone suggests a round-robin where each person builds on the previous person's solution. How do you engage?

You're someone who prefers to work independently and handle things on your own terms. Your partner suggests you jointly manage the household budget by reviewing expenses together each week. How do you respond?
